item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is it. The worst thing I’ve ever seen

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …That bit with Mr. Meme breaking into uncontrollable laughter whenever he sees the Gamers' name or is asked about them is a failsafe, isn't it? To keep him from being interrogated effectively?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is good. The character is interesting and sympathetic, I really like the idea of an anomalous person who isn't a terrifying freak whose life was substantially improved by being captured by the Foundation. They're friendly to him, they're giving him a basic education and meaningful work that he enjoys, and they're even seriously considering promoting him to an agent someday. It pleases me to see the Foundation not being unnessarilly cruel and grimdark.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: what's wrong with it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Still one of the best MTF names on the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The writing and development was really good, and I really enjoyed it, but "Nine-Tailed Dox"… Really?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, it's been a while since I've last read this, and upon re-reading I still stand by most of my points, except for the length thing. As the article is right now its length is just fine. It's what is accomplished in that length, or rather, what isn't accomplished, that bothers me.

+ extended review
– hide block

The characterization for Mr. Meme is rather weak, for instance. He's just a generic 21 year old dude for the most part, nothing really makes me care about him as a character. Nothing much is done with the idea of a GOI that decided to take the Little Misters and remake them for the 'hip youngsters crowd' in this article either.

For instance, take the whole bit about him being used to aid in finding POI. He's feeling kind of lethargic about having nothing to do, so he makes a request. There's a short interview log that only really has one relevant line (the one explaining that), and the rest doesn't really add anything to the concept or the character either.

So, maybe it's better to say what I want to see out of this guy. I want him to be weirder, in whatever way he can be. Go into how bizarre it is that this guy exists in the first place, go into the whole meme thing and try to make that funny, I don't know, just anything that makes this not be just a dude with a barely anomalous ability.

On top of that, this guy is called Mr. Meme! The last thing that this article should be is boring, since memes are never that. They can be funny, they can be cringeworthy, they can be infuriating when they're used wrong but they are pretty much never boring. That should be reflected in the feel of the article in one way or another.

All in all I'm keeping the downvote in spite of how solid the writing is technically, which is still true. But if it isn't interesting that all just feels like a waste of effort.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Stanky maymays!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Latepost, but even though I know the "some kid" that Mr. Meme wakes up to was probably his buyer from GAW, I want to headcanon that the kid is a GAW affiliate, and that GAW's dankness and immaturity is explained by them being run and staffed entirely by fourteen-year-olds.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad you enjoyed the article! Doctor Steele is named after my high school chemistry teacher.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As someone who loves the internet and how chaotic yet ordered it all is, I love this new group. The character development, the items, the fact that they are parodying another GOI is all amazing to me.

+ Extra Stuff
- Title for hiding text

I assume Doctor Steele has nothing to do with the OTHER Doctor Steel, but it's nice to think it does.
There is a Mister Monty Python And The Holy Grail, and someone needs to make him a thing that exists. I don't care if it's a tale, GOI format, or an actual SCP, just MAKE IT HAPPEN! Please.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was going to ask that myself until I saw the two numbers next to each other in your post.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This isn't a Dr. Wondertainment Little Mister, either. Its a rip-off/parody by GAW.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, it is not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So in regards to the document attached at the bottom of the page, Mr. Bernie Sanders is SCP-2433, right? Can we add that link to the list in the Wondertainment document?

(Love how this all ties in with everything, by the way. Awesome job.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: they were removed for having too much swag

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
04. Mr. Get Anything For Free In Any Shop
20. Mr. Sex Number

Question: What happened to nos. 05 to 19?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's missing a necessary Mr.
Mr. High Quality

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just noting here for posterity that, with anqxyr's permission, the tag gamers-against-weed has been added to all extant articles that refer to or feature the group.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He finds humorous things though. Doesn't necessarily encompass all memes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: it's a meme u dip

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
if you're being serious, breakaway sect of anartists disenfranchised with the sociopathic nature of many awcy practitioners

if not, accelerando is right

pakoosh

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At first I though "living embodiment of 9gag", but the interviews put a new spin on it. A guy who can steal memes in Foundation custody is practically Thaumiel given how many memetic anomalies there are. And as indifferent as I was about GAW at first, I really like where it's going now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
here I'll answer all those questions in one go

A meme.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wtf is GAW? Why did this happen? What even is happening? What have we become?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Headcanon: Dr. Wondertainment knows about this guy and GAW. He thinks both are hilarious scamps, and is a little bit flattered.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this one a lot. As someone else said, it was a good character piece. It feels like you have just enough explanation of the anomaly before going into a fairly mundane but charming exploration of the concept. I love the idea that he's helping find persons of interest based on what they would find funny; that could use some more expansion, but it's perfectly workable as is. It just has me imagining, like, Sarkic "joke" memes (involving a lot of meat and blood) or Maxwellian gif jokes about the cogwork orthodox or unaugmented, or the completly incomprehensible mishmash that a Directorate K member would like. "Hahaha, green taxis! Oh man, can you imagine?"

+1, and I'm off to read the others.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Goddammit, sorry. I read both skips in quick succession and didn't think as hard as I should have before posting, apparently.

In any case, I do still look forward to seeing where the Misters Against Weed drafts are going, even if it doesn't mesh with my initial impression of the GOI!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the various people in the chatlog; trying to differentiate a bunch of internet personas can be tricky but you nailed it, imo.

… are you sure this bit is about this SCP, and not SCP-2293? I didn't write that one.

Anyways, I hate to disappoint you, but I personally know of three other Misters Against Weed drafts in the works, once of which is mine. If you want to see internet people putting ridiculous amounts of effort into producing incredibly silly parodies of something precious few people care about anyways, check out old montage parodies (examples if you're not photosensitive: 1, 2, 3, 4. There are more but they're 3edgy5me). I think it's completely in-character for communities like GAW to take jokes incredibly seriously.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A wholehearted +1. I like that it sounds like a SCP-J, but no, it's literally just an SCP that is also a joke. I liked the characterization of both the skip himself and the various people in the chatlog; trying to differentiate a bunch of internet personas can be tricky but you nailed it, imo.

One thing: I really hope that we DON'T get any more "Gamer Misters", 'cause I think it'd kind of ruin the joke out of this one? Like, from my current read on Gamers Against Weed (which I love and do want to see more of) them doing a one-off parody of Wondertainment and writing a document taking the complete piss out of them seems much more, I dunno, fitting than actually taking the effort to follow through and create a "Mr. Bernie Sanders."

…Are We Gamers Yet?

item.title: Hmm....
item.content:encoded: Sounds like a Joke Scp more than an actual one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting jerk-offs.

Also this is much more self-aggrandizing than either of those, since it's basically Scan shoving half his personal hang-ups and in-jokes into an article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To be fair, if this can be called "jerking off onto the SCP template", what would it make the former library and the one that is styled as notes?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was going to downvote this for basically just being Scantron jerking off onto the SCP template, but about halfway through it actually became a fairly interesting character piece, so +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh, I figured that GAW was an a new 'edgy' product line from Dr. Wondertainment, trying to remain relevant to millenials…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think this counts as trolling.

28. Mr. Ominous (discontinued)

While this made me laugh, this isn't a -J. I was already salty about the entirety of the Little Misters being turned into articles; we don't need more. Plus, we've already got Senor Purple. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm excited to see where this leads.

I felt it was a little bit long, but it was an enjoyable enough read. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, having each of the Misters Against Weed working for different GOIs would be a great idea, I would like to see that!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: holy shit that title kills me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This… I like this. The SCP itself was interesting enough, with good characterization for Mr. Meme, but the list (and inauguration of a new series of Misters) really sets this apart. I'm really looking forward to seeing what people do with these, and I may even dust off my seldom-used writer's cap for one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As far as I'm concerned, no. The Misters Against Weed could be a good opportunity to write Little Misters situated in GOI formats or tales IMO.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At this rate, is there any actual restriction that the Mister Gamers (or whatever they're called) have to be in the SCP format?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well I was considering having Mr. Original Character working for ORIA…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pretty much fully agreeing with this, although I'm just gonna novote.

Also,

MTF Omicron-12, "Nine-Tailed Dox"

You are a bastard man and I hate you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, what I get from this is that somebody is trying to take Little Misters and make them "cool with the hip, happening youths of today with their facetubes and snapograms".

Sign me the fuck up. +1'd with gusto.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: (accidental double post, please delete)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to admit, while I love this as its own thing, what this really gets me excited for is "Little Misters: Generation II"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If it's parody, does that mean that Mr. Litigation can't get involved (assuming GAW operates out of the US or other countries with parody protection laws)? Or will we get Mr. Original Character vs. Mr. Litigation in a cool tale?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry that this didn't work for you, and thanks for leaving detailed feedback anyways.

I'm always open to the possibility that my articles are too drawn out — if you can believe it, the first draft was even longer, with even less characterization. Striking the right balance for length is where I struggle most as a writer, tbqh.

Did you find that there were any parts in particular that felt like a slog to get through, or that didn't add enough to justify their inclusion?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Glad you enjoyed it!

Now, I am aware GAW is suposed to rip the hell off Wondertainment, but was it so necessary to add a discontinued Mr Ominous?

+ show block
– hide block

I can go over my headcanoned motivations and ideas for Gamers Against Weed if you're interested, but let's examine the SCP itself to see what we get.

The list is unmistakably a reference to Wondertainment, and imitates him in a lot of aspects (up to and including lifting text from the Little Misters note). They admit to this, but instead of "Based on Dr. Wondertainment's Little Misters" or "Not related to Dr. Wondertainment's Little Misters", it's "Who is Dr. Wondertainment?"

Now, GAW obviously knows the Dr. Wondertainment brand thoroughly, to the point where they would create an actual human being in its image. They ask 'Who is Dr. Wondertainment?" as a way of dismissing him: he is, symbolically, beneath GAW's notice. The message is "we can do it better, we don't take your work seriously, and we don't even take ourselves seriously". Given how Wondertainment is particularly protective of its intellectual property, it's a pretty big slap in the face too.

It's not a rip-off, it's a parody — and not a particularly friendly one either. Riffing on as many elements of Wondertainment's style as possible, and subjectively improving upon his products where they can, makes the parody clearer and stronger. With Mr. Ominous, they're calling attention to the fact that putting a 'discontinued' tag next to the guy with the weird name is, well, ominous as shit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmnn, this is a difficult one. On the one hand, the writing is pretty solid, but on the other hand nothing really stands out about it. On the one hand this is about the best you can get out of this idea, but on the other hand this idea really isn't good to begin with.

Sorry, but this will be a downvote for me. This might have worked better if it wasn't so overly long - none of the addenda really add that much to this guy. They do need to exist for this to be interesting beyond the basics of 'what does he do' but they're just too long. But most of all this just doesn't grab me, and I'm not sure you really can do anything with this concept to make it more interesting than it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly? It's refreshing and it gives this (sometimes necessary) feel that anomalies (and the website's writing properly adjust to our current culture from time to time. In fact, this skip is pretty human, which is good, and the article provides a plausible reason in-universe to allow it to help without falling into self-insertness1 Now, I am aware GAW is suposed to rip the hell off Wondertainment, but was it so necessary to add a discontinued Mr Ominous?

TL;DR: Neat, nice characterization and everthing works well. +1.

Footnotes
1. I'm not taking in count that you used to be a shitposting machine Scan.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want to make my own Gamers Against Weed SCP now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just make sure they Oolong Johnson while popping the wheelie.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So we got the real thing, a Hispanic knockoff of the real thing, and whatever this is.

And I agree with psul that this should be the only one of its kind because this whole internet culture schtick can get old real fast.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I suppose that could be interesting if used sparingly, but having them ALL be like that would just get samey and overly restrictive. Giving them all the desire to use their anomalous properties, I can get behind that, but if they're different personalities I don't see why they would all want to or be able to work for the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm very uncertain of how I feel about this. I suspect that it would work better if this was the only one of its series, but that is unlikely to be the case, knowing the site's propensities. As such, I would advocate that, as well as having to work with the names, each of the Misters Against Weed articles should have the Mister working for the Foundation in some capacity, as is the case here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
tuxedo guy holding a sewing machine in front of a crashed truck

I'm really more of a "sick wheelie in front of a blowjob and dogs" guy personally

item.title: I'll make my own Little Misters! With references! And memes! Actually, forget the references.
item.content:encoded:
Image source: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Nick_Carter_mugshot.jpg Public domain.

Anyways, this is my spin on the Little Misters. Instead of trying to build an SCP out of a vague descriptor, try to squeeze one out of these rather weird and specific descriptors. Restrictions breed creativity, after all. If you write your own Mister Against Weed (or anything else by Gamers Against Weed) and want my help or input, feel free to message me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gamers Against Weed is definitely a GoI that I can get into. I like this scip, because I think it holds up as something actually interesting while also being fairly funny. And I think that's what makes this a main-lister more than a -J. It answers your "Really?" with a strong affirmative and takes everything seriously. I'm into Project Crowdread. I'm happy for the meme boy.
